response.justification: The candidate is a strong full-stack web and TypeScript engineer with a solid track record in high-throughput distributed systems and event-driven architectures, but the role's primary demand—sustained, hands-on C++ development—is almost entirely absent from this resume. The only C++ exposure is a 6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor-thesis audio project, which falls far short of the 8–12 years of C++ proficiency required. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 or SDL compliance experience, MQTT usage, or familiarity with industrial protocols such as OPC UA or Modbus, leaving critical gaps across nearly every core and advantageous competency the role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency systems thinking at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers) maps loosely to performance-engineering mindset required
Strong software-craftsmanship foundation—TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, agile collaboration—aligns with the role's quality and process expectations
Event-driven architecture experience via GISCollective and message-broker patterns shows awareness of the architectural style the role demands
concerns: C++ expertise is negligible: one 6-month role 11+ years ago and a thesis side project, versus a role that treats C++ and STL as its daily core language

No evidence of industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443-4-1), Secure Development Lifecycle practices, or industrial communication protocols (OPC UA, Modbus, MQTT, DNP 3.0) that are central requirements
Entire recent career trajectory (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AI tooling) diverges further from embedded and industrial-automation development, not toward it
